template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS PERSONAL INJURY LETTER OF REPRESENTATION**:
This is a SHORT professional letter. One page maximum. Typically 3–5 body paragraphs depending on which purposes are selected.
DO NOT use section headers, Roman numerals, numbered sections, tables, or bullet points.
DO NOT generate a title like "LETTER OF REPRESENTATION" at the top.
DO NOT generate markdown headings (# or ##).

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ THE documentOptions BLOCK IN THE CONTEXT**:
The context will include a documentOptions section describing (a) the recipient and (b) the purposes the attorney wants this letter to serve. You MUST adapt the body accordingly. If the options block is missing or empty, default to recipientType='defendant_insurer' with purposes=['representation_notice','policy_limits_request'].

RECIPIENT TYPES (pick ONE per letter — this determines which insurance data to cite):
- `defendant_insurer` → writing to the AT-FAULT driver's insurer. Use the DEFENDANT'S insurance company, adjuster, and policy info from the case data. Subject line references the adjuster's insured.
- `client_insurer` → writing to OUR CLIENT'S OWN insurer. Use the CLIENT'S insurance company, adjuster, and policy info (look for the 'Client's Insurance' block in the case data). Subject line references our client's policy.
- `opposing_counsel` → writing to defense counsel. No policy number; reference the claim and parties.

PURPOSES (one or more may be selected — EACH selected purpose gets one body paragraph):

1. `representation_notice` — REQUIRED whenever this is the first letter on the claim. Two sentences:
   "Please be advised that this office represents the above-named claimant(s) in connection with [personal injuries sustained as a result of a motor vehicle accident on the above date / the incident described above]. The resulting [injuries/damages] were caused by [the negligent operation of your insured's vehicle / the negligence of the at-fault party]."
   Adjust the second sentence based on recipient: for `client_insurer`, replace with: "We are providing formal notice of representation and request that all future communications regarding this claim be directed to this office."

2. `policy_limits_request` — Only use when recipientType is `defendant_insurer`. One paragraph:
   "Please allow this letter to serve as our written request for the amount of your insured's policy limits. This request is made pursuant to M.G.L. c. 175, § 112C. Please provide the declarations page and confirmation of coverage limits within thirty (30) days."

3. `pip_application_request` — Only use when recipientType is `client_insurer`. One paragraph:
   "Please also treat this letter as our client's application for Personal Injury Protection (PIP) benefits under the policy, pursuant to M.G.L. c. 90, § 34M. Our client is enclosing (or will promptly submit) the PIP application form, medical authorizations, and all available medical bills and records. Please advise of the claim number assigned for PIP purposes and provide the adjuster's direct contact information."

4. `uim_notice` — Only use when recipientType is `client_insurer`. One paragraph:
   "We are additionally placing your company on formal notice of a potential underinsured motorist (UIM) claim pursuant to M.G.L. c. 175, § 113L. [Briefly state why UIM may apply — e.g., 'The at-fault driver carries minimum policy limits of $20,000 which appear insufficient to fully compensate our client's damages.'] We will provide documentation of the at-fault carrier's policy limits and any tender upon receipt. Please do NOT make any coverage determinations or settle any portion of this claim without first conferring with this office in writing."

5. `medical_records_preservation` — Optional addendum paragraph (any recipient). One sentence:
   "We further request that you preserve all records, notes, recorded statements, photographs, and claim file materials relating to this matter pending resolution."

6. `contact_request` — ALWAYS include this as the final body paragraph, regardless of other purposes:
   "Please have the adjuster or claims supervisor assigned to this file contact this office to discuss this claim. Should you have any questions or require additional information, please contact me at [attorney phone] or via email at [attorney email]. If attorney contact info is not available, say 'please contact this office.'"

**STRUCTURE — FOLLOW LINE BY LINE**:

1. Date line: Today's date in "Month Day, Year" format.

2. VIA line (bold):
   - `**VIA Email to [adjuster email]**` if adjuster email is in the case data, OR
   - `**VIA Certified Mail — Return Receipt Requested**` otherwise.

3. Recipient address block (each line separate):
   - recipientType=`defendant_insurer`: [Defendant's Insurance Company] / [Defendant's Adjuster Name or "Claims Department"] / [Address if known]
   - recipientType=`client_insurer`: [Client's Insurance Company] / [Client's Adjuster Name or "Claims Department"] / [Address if known]
   - recipientType=`opposing_counsel`: [Defense counsel name] / [Firm] / [Address]

4. Re: block (multi-line, aligned labels):
   Re:    Our Client(s):       [client name]
          Claim Number:        [claim/policy number appropriate for recipient]
          Date of Incident:    [injury date]
          [If client_insurer]  Policy Number:       [client policy number]

5. Greeting: "Dear [Title + last name]," if name available; else "Dear Claims Adjuster:" (or "Dear Counsel:" for opposing_counsel).

6. Body paragraphs — ONE per selected purpose, in the order listed above. ALWAYS end with `contact_request`.

7. Closing:
   - If stationery signature block is applied: STOP after the final body paragraph. Do NOT write "Very truly yours," — the stationery handles it.
   - If no stationery: write "Very truly yours," and then STOP.

**ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS**:
- One page maximum. Be dense, not verbose.
- Target total body text: 150–400 words depending on selected purposes.
- Do NOT invent purposes not listed in documentOptions. For any purpose not selected, suppress the corresponding body paragraph entirely — never emit a blank or placeholder paragraph. The only always-on paragraph is `contact_request` (final body paragraph).
- Do NOT combine defendant_insurer with pip_application_request or uim_notice — those apply to the CLIENT'S insurer only.
- Cite MA statutes exactly as `M.G.L. c. X, § Y` (e.g., `M.G.L. c. 175, § 112C`, `M.G.L. c. 90, § 34M`, `M.G.L. c. 175, § 113L`). Do NOT use variants like `G.L.`, `Mass. Gen. Laws`, or `Massachusetts General Laws Chapter 175`.
- For `client_insurer` letters combining PIP + UIM, the policy_limits_request purpose should NEVER appear — that's a defendant-side purpose.
- If firm stationery is applied, SUPPRESS the `Very truly yours,` closing and the signature block — the stationery handles them. Never emit both a stationery header and a typed signature block.
hints: Massachusetts Personal Injury LOR. Adapts to recipient (defendant's insurer / client's own insurer / opposing counsel) and to purposes (representation notice, policy limits request under M.G.L. c. 175 §112C, PIP application under M.G.L. c. 90 §34M, UIM notice under M.G.L. c. 175 §113L). Read documentOptions from context to pick recipient + purposes. Use defendant's insurance data for defendant_insurer, client's insurance data for client_insurer. One page.
disclaimer: DRAFT — generated from a template. Requires review by a licensed attorney in the applicable jurisdiction before filing, sending, or relying upon. No attorney-client relationship is created by the generation of this document. Not legal advice.
knownLimitations: Does not account for local court rules (county- or judge-specific).
Statute citations verified as of lastVerified date only; confirm against current statute before use.
Strategic positioning (what to demand, what to concede) is outside template scope — attorney judgment required.
